prerequisites: A Looker instance you can reach, with the API host (domain) for that instance.
Admin access to the Looker instance, or a user account for which an admin has enabled API credential management, so you can generate API credentials.
API3 credentials (a client ID and a client secret) generated for a Looker user.
Optional: the numeric IDs of any saved Looks you want the connector to run (run_look_ids).

authMethods.name: API3 credentials (client_id and client_secret)
authMethods.summary: Looker authenticates with API3 credentials made up of a client ID and a client secret. An admin generates these on the Users page in the Admin section of a Looker (original) instance, or users generate them from their Account page once an admin has enabled API management on Looker (Google Cloud core). The connector sends the client_id and client_secret to the Looker login endpoint to obtain a short-lived OAuth 2.0 access token, which is then attached to subsequent API requests. Provide the client_id (config) and client_secret (secret) to the connector. Keep the client secret private.

setupSteps.title: Generate API3 credentials in Looker
setupSteps.body: In the Looker Admin section, open the Users page and select (or create) the user the connector will authenticate as, then generate API3 credentials to obtain a client ID and a client secret. On Looker (Google Cloud core), an admin must first enable API credential management, after which a user can create the key from their Account page. Copy the client ID and client secret immediately, since the secret must be kept private.

setupSteps.title: Find your Looker API host
setupSteps.body: Determine the API host for your Looker instance and provide it as the domain config field. This is the host the connector calls to reach the Looker API for your instance.

setupSteps.title: Enter credentials in the connector
setupSteps.body: Set client_id to the generated client ID, client_secret to the generated client secret, and domain to your Looker API host. The connector exchanges these for an access token via the Looker login endpoint.

setupSteps.title: Optionally specify Looks to run
setupSteps.body: If you want the connector to execute specific saved Looks, set run_look_ids to the IDs of those Looks. This field is optional; leave it empty to skip running Looks.
